popularStory.headline: Summary of King William I
popularStory.description:
Name: King William I (The Lion) of Scotland
Father: Henry, Earl of Northumberland
Mother: Ada de Warenne
House of: Canmore
Born: c. 1142/43
Ascended to the throne: December 9, 1165
Crowned: December 2 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William 'The Lion' King of Scotland於大約1143年出生在蘇格蘭培爾培斯時，他的父親Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria30歲，母親Ada de Warenne24歲。他於1189年9月5日與Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland在英國英格蘭奧可福爾武德斯托可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1174年登記服兵役。他去世於1214年12月4日在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年72歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭安格斯阿布洛阿斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William 'The Lion' King of Scotland于大约1143年出生在苏格兰培爾培斯时，他的父亲Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria30岁，母亲Ada de Warenne24岁。他于1189年9月5日与Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland在英国英格兰奧可福爾武德斯托可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1174年登记服兵役。他去世于1214年12月4日在苏格兰斯林史爾斯特林享年72岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰安格斯阿布洛阿斯。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Scotland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Kinross and Clackmannanshire):
from the Middle English personal name Scotland, Scolland. Found in Normandy as well as England, it evidently denoted a Scot or someone with an association with Scotland.
ethnic name for someone from Scotland, from Middle English Scotlond ‘Scotland’. Compare England .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: 'The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Lion'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Derivative of   Leo  , from the oblique case. This form is common as a Jewish name, but recently has also acquired wider currency. The lion is an important symbol among Jews because of Jacob's dying pronouncement that ‘Judah is a lion's whelp’ (Genesis 49:9).

en.lifeSummary: When William 'The Lion' King of Scotland was born about 1143, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, his father, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, was 30 and his mother, Ada de Warenne, was 24. He married Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland on 5 September 1189, in Woodstock, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1174. He died on 4 December 1214, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 72, and was buried in Arbroath, Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William 'The Lion' King of Scotland est né environ 1143, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ada de Warenne, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland le 5 septembre 1189, à Woodstock, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1174. Il est décédé le 4 décembre 1214, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Arbroath, Angus, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William 'The Lion' King of Scotland um 1143 in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria 30 und seine Mutter Ada de Warenne 24. Er hat am 5. September 1189 in Woodstock, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1174 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 4. Dezember 1214 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in Arbroath, Angus, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland il 5 settembre 1189, nel Woodstock, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1174. Morì il 4 dicembre 1214, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Arbroath, Angus, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William 'The Lion' King of Scotlandが約1143年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで生まれた時，父Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbriaは30歳，母Ada de Warenneは24歳であった。1189年9月5日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアウッドストックでErmengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1174年に兵役登録をした。彼は1214年12月4日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで72歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアンガスアルブロアスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William 'The Lion' King of Scotland(이)가 대략 1143년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada de Warenne(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1189년 9월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 우드스톡에서 Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1174년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1214년 12월 4일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스 아브로스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William 'The Lion' King of Scotland nasceu aproximadamente 1143, em Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, seu pai, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Ada de Warenne, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland em 5 de setembro de 1189, em Woodstock, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1174. Ele faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1214, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Arbroath, Angus, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William 'The Lion' King of Scotland родился около 1143 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada de Warenne, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland 5 сентября 1189 г. в местности вудстокк, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1174 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 4 декабря 1214 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности арброат, ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.from 9 December 1165 to 4 December 1214: desde 9 de diciembre de 1165 hasta 4 de diciembre de 1214
es.Blair Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland: Blair Atholl, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando William 'The Lion' King of Scotland nació aproximadamente 1143, en Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, su papá, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ada de Warenne, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Ermengarde de Beaumont Queen of Scotland el 5 de septiembre de 1189, en Woodstock, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1174. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1214, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Arbroath, Angus, Escocia, Reino Unido.
